question_text: A 2 kg block is pushed across a wooden floor with an applied force of 15 N. The coefficient of friction between the block and floor is 0.4. (g = 10 m/s²) Calculate: (a) the friction force acting on the block, (b) the net force on the block, and (c) the acceleration of the block.
correct_answer: (a) Friction force = 8 N; (b) Net force = 7 N; (c) Acceleration = 3.5 m/s²
explanation: First, calculate friction using F_friction = μ × N = 0.4 × (2 × 10) = 8 N. Then find net force by subtracting friction from applied force: 15 - 8 = 7 N. Finally, use F = ma to get acceleration: a = 7 ÷ 2 = 3.5 m/s².
